qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Forced development mode and hardcoded DevGate credentials expose debug output to unauthenticated users
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about installer-forced development mode plus hardcoded DevGate Basic Auth credentials exposing debug output. The advisory is a Zip Slip in `modules/Theme/Controllers/Theme.php::upload()` caused by extracting unvalidated ZIP entry paths, so the vulnerable code and root cause are different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable `Theme::upload` archive-extraction path or an equivalent Zip Slip arbitrary file write condition.
evidence: The advisory describes `modules/Theme/Controllers/Theme.php:13-56` calling `ZipArchive::extractTo()` on attacker-controlled ZIP contents without validating entry names, enabling authenticated theme-upload Zip Slip to arbitrary file write and PHP file drop RCE.

The only final qualifying finding in the session manifest is `devgate-default-creds-debug-disclosure`, whose cited code locations are `modules/Backend/Commands/Ci4msSetup.php`, `modules/Install/Controllers/Install.php`, `modules/DevGate/Config/Registrar.php`, `modules/DevGate/Config/DevGate.php`, `modules/DevGate/Filters/DevGateFilter.php`, and `app/Filters/Ci4ms.php`.

That finding's exploit path is DevGate Basic Auth bypass with shipped credentials leading to development-mode exception disclosure, not ZIP upload, archive entry traversal, arbitrary filesystem write, or `Theme::upload`.
